summary: Backend-only end-to-end testing for Fazgom Togo. All 34 pytest tests passed (100% success). Covered: auth (register/login/OTP/me + phone normalization 8 digits -> +228xxxxxxxx), profile update, KYC encrypted upload (assistant only, JPG/PNG/PDF, ciphertext never returned in /kyc/my), cash entries CRUD + summary (today/week/month), stock CRUD + adjust + low-stock alerts, credits with auto-paid status, reports flash (cash + stock_value + credits_due + 14-day daily_series), missions marketplace full lifecycle (merchant create -> feed visible to assistant -> apply -> duplicate apply blocked -> merchant lists applicants -> accept -> mock payment funds escrow + status in_progress -> messages bidirectional + conversations -> complete with 7% commission payout (10000 -> 9300 net, 700 commission) -> reviews bidirectional + rating recompute -> premium subscribe -> earnings). Verified no ObjectId (_id) leaks across 9 list/object endpoints by recursive key check.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~935 lines; consider splitting into routers (auth, kyc, cash, stock, credits, missions, payments, messages, reviews) for maintainability before adding more features.

KYC encryption key, JWT secret are in backend/.env - acceptable for now; for production, move to secret manager and rotate KYC_ENCRYPTION_KEY_B64 (currently a static 32-byte key). Document a key-rotation strategy because existing ciphertexts cannot be decrypted with a new key.
/api/payments/init for real FedaPay flow is a stub (returns checkout_url: None). Acceptable since FEDAPAY_API_KEY is empty (mock active), but flag for when going live.

/api/missions/{id}/complete releases escrow and creates a payout regardless of whether the assistant has done the work; consider an explicit 'submit_work' step from the assistant before merchant validation, or a dispute window.
Premium subscription is a one-shot toggle - premium_expires_at is set but never enforced anywhere in the code; missions feed/applications sort by is_premium without checking expiry.

Mission 'release escrow' path mutates payouts then mission; not transactional. With MongoDB, if the second update fails, payout exists with mission still assigned. Consider idempotency keys / status checks before insert (released flag guards reasonably well).
normalize_phone has an edge case: passing a phone like '0090111111' (10 digits, non-Togo) becomes '+0090111111' - not Togo. Validation should reject non +228 numbers if Togo-only is the rule.
/api/messages permission logic: an assistant who applied but was REJECTED can still send messages (only check is 'applied'). Should restrict to accepted applicant or merchant once assigned.
OTP send is unauthenticated and unrate-limited; anyone can spam OTPs for any phone. Add IP-based or phone-based rate-limit (e.g., 1 per 60s, 5 per hour).
Auth /api/auth/me protected by HTTPBearer auto_error=True: missing token returns 403 from FastAPI's default; convention is 401. Cosmetic only - tests pass for 401/403.
test_credentials: Tests generate unique phones each run using a timestamp tag: 9{tag}1 (merchant) and 9{tag}2 (assistant), password TestPass123! - normalized to +228xxxxxxxx by the API. No fixed test users used; /app/memory/test_credentials.md not present.
seed_data_creation: Per-run ephemeral users/data prefixed with 'TEST '. No cleanup performed (no admin endpoints); test phones are unique per run so they don't collide.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully functional end-to-end. Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_fazgom_e2e.py - it generates fresh users each run via timestamp-based phones. If frontend testing is requested next, focus on: register/login flow with OTP dev_code display, merchant mission creation, assistant feed + apply, payment mock UX, mission chat, completion + review, premium subscription. Pay attention to data-testid coverage on forms/buttons.
rca of the issue: Initial 1/34 failure was a false-positive in the testing script itself: substring 'in' was used to check 'no _id leak' but matched user_id. Fixed by recursive JSON key walk. Server code is clean - no real ObjectId leaks detected.
